title: Responsible Disclosure Protocol
author: Chew, Kean Ho
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Do you find a potential vulnerability but lack a clear path to resolve it? Do you fear legal retaliation from the owner or collateral parties? This continuously researched responsible disclosure protocol offers a structured resolution path. We first surveyed existing practices from the technology sector's Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) framework. We then adapted these practices for other industrial applications. 
 Continuous updates define this protocol. We revise the protocol periodically based on new case studies. Each chapter progresses step by step, which makes the document functionally purposeful. This protocol does not prescribe rigid rules. Instead, it consolidates procedures you can reference and modify according to your situation. 
 Under no circumstances does any project team assume responsibility for consequences, whether intended or unintended. You should implement this protocol only after you fully comprehend, agree to, and ratify its terms. When you deal with a vulnerability, life threatening risk may arise if the owner acts with malicious intent. 
 This document provides a living framework for responsible disclosure, not a legal guarantee. We encourage you to adapt the protocol with professional judgment and legal counsel where appropriate.
